<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghast Stiletto](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4419)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _ghast stiletto_ is a _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ wrapped in the still-undead skin of a [ghast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=219). While you wield the dagger, you gain a +1 status bonus to all saves against [paralysis](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The _ghast stiletto_ afflicts the next living target struck by the dagger with a terrible wound that emits the foul stench of the grave. For the next minute, the target creature, as well as any creatures within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (plus [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as long as it’s sickened on a critical failure). While within the emanation, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include skin from a [ghast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=219).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghast Stiletto

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scizore of the Crab](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4420)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn on 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Scizore](/Weapons.aspx?ID=303)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _scizore of the crab_ is a _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [scizore](/Weapons.aspx?ID=303)_ that has the [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619) trait in addition to its normal weapon traits.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** Your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with this weapon, or you have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) with this weapon; **Effect** The target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) until the end of your next turn. A grabbed creature can use the [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) action to get out of the Grab, and the Grab ends for a grabbed creature if you move away from it. This functions as the [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) creature ability.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** The crab claw animates and pinches open and shut, dealing slashing damage equal to the _scizore of the crab’s_ number of damage dice.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a claw from a [giant crab](/Monsters.aspx?ID=588) or similar creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scizore of the Crab

</title>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [wounding ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)_ is studded with teeth pulled from a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480), which usually requires an animate donor, given vampires’ tendency to turn to dust when destroyed.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;  **Requirements** Your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with this weapon and you’re not in direct sunlight; **Effect** The _vampire-fang morningstar_ absorbs blood from the target, healing the wielder for 10 Hit Points.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include teeth from a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vine Whip](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4422)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 38

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404)_ is crafted from the vine of a dangerous plant creature. It deals bludgeoning or slashing damage, according to the vine attack of the creature it was harvested from. For example, collecting a vine from an [assassin vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555) would result in a _vine whip_ that deals bludgeoning damage, while one from a [mandragora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=727) or [viper vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=854) would deal piercing damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;    **Requirements** You have soil or sand within your reach; **Effect** You [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) the weapon and plant it in the ground, where it takes root and fights on its own against the last enemy you attacked or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated.

The weapon has a space of 5 feet, acts on your initiative, and gains two actions. It doesn’t block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375). The weapon can’t use reactions, and it acts on your turn. 

While Activated, a _vine whip_ makes Strikes with an attack modifier of +22 plus its item bonus to attack rolls (normally +24 total). It uses the weapon’s normal damage but has a +0 Strength modifier. The whip’s abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can’t be Activated or benefit from any of your abilities while rooted.

Each round, when the _vine whip_ is finished using its actions, attempt a DC 6 flat check. On a failure, the Activation ends and the _vine whip_ becomes inanimate. You can’t Activate the item again for 10 minutes. Removing the _vine whip_ from the ground takes an Interact action. 

While rooted, the _vine whip_ gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 1.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the vine from a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creature with a vine attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vine Whip

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kaldemash's Lament](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4423)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Resembling little more than a simple iron pipe with a handle, _Kaldemash’s Lament_ is one of the most well-known star guns in all of Arcadia. Legends state the Crowned Regent Kaldemash helped forge one of the first star guns millennia ago. While the star gun served Kaldemash as a powerful weapon, its most notable achievement was the accidental killing of one of Kaldemash’s greatest friends. This death is what caused the regent to recognize the true destructive power of the star guns and led to him developing the Star Code, a set of rules of engagement and proper use of firearms still in use in Arcadia today. Although Kaldemash never named the weapon himself, all legends that mention the weapon refer to it as _Kaldemash’s Lament_.

The legendary weapon is a _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2844) advanced firearm_ with a range increment of 90 feet. It deals 4d6 force damage (with the _major striking_ rune included) and has the concealable, concussive, and fatal d10 traits. You don’t take a penalty when dealing nonlethal damage with the weapon. Like most star guns, _Kaldemash’s Lament_ uses magic to function and doesn’t require ammunition or black powder.

If you use _Kaldemash’s Lament_ as part of a duel in which all parties are in agreement on the terms, the gun’s supernatural instincts help you make the quickest draw. You roll twice and take the higher result on your initiative roll for the duel; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. In addition, you can draw _Kaldemash’s Lament_ as a free action at the start of your turn during the duel. If you attempt to fire the star gun in bad faith at a dueling opponent once they have surrendered, been defeated, or the duel is over, _Kaldemash’s Lament_ flies out of your hand and you can’t pick it up, hold it, or wield it for 10 minutes.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** You target a creature with an attack;  **Effect** You adjust the damage that _Kaldemash’s Lament_ deals before firing. _Kaldemash’s Lament’s_ damage type changes to either [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) until you change the type again.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You leave _Kaldemash’s Lament_ to fire on its own. You release the star gun and it begins to move independently, flying through the air and firing. You still gain the benefits of the gun’s [_speed_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2844) rune while it’s moving independently. It has a space of 5 feet but doesn’t block or impede enemies attempting to move through that space. It always remains within 30 feet of you and intentionally resists being taken or otherwise moved; all attempts to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) it fail. _Kaldemash’s Lament_ moves this way for 3 rounds, after which it returns to your hand. If you don’t have a free hand to hold it, the gun instead holsters itself on your person. 

While _Kaldemash’s Lament_ is moving independently, you can command it to make a Strike against a creature as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. It makes a ranged Strike using your attack modifier while wielding it or a +31 bonus, whichever is higher. This attack increases your multiple attack penalty as normal, and the gun uses your multiple attack penalty when determining its attack bonus. Since the star gun is constantly moving and attempting to line up the appropriate shot, creatures it targets are [flat‑footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the gun’s attacks.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** You unleash a barrage of attacks in an instant. You deal Strike damage to all creatures in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This barrage counts as three attacks for your multiple attack penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kaldemash's Lament

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518)_ is used by caravan guards to nonlethally—though powerfully—deter large game and bandits. When fired, the spark gun deals mental damage and adds the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait to the attack. Each _mindlance_ also includes a [reinforced stock](/Weapons.aspx?ID=187) that benefits from any fundamental runes on the firearm. When you critically succeed at an attack roll with a _mindlance_, the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 unless it succeeds at a DC 24 Will save.

>
[Solar Shellflower](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4425)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 39

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Flintlock Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=521)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=521)_ features multiple triangular panels that can be folded out of the stock, like the petals of a tigridia flower, that collect sunlight and feed it into the spark gun’s core. All damage dealt by a _solar shellflower_ is fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day (but see below);   **Effect** One of the panels from the _solar shellflower_ detaches and unfurls into a tigridia-shaped construct of flame. For the next 1 minute, whenever you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) an enemy with the _solar shellflower_, the construct fires a smaller jet of fire that automatically hits the target, dealing 3d4 additional [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) or twice that on a critical hit. The _solar shellflower_ usually requires a full day to replenish enough solar energy to recharge this Activation, but if used in an environment with especially strong sunlight, such as in a desert, cliff above the clouds, or near the summer solstice, it can recharge in 1 hour instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Solar Shellflower

Arcadian gunslingers liken the rotating cores to a group of dancers, each taking their turn in the spotlight.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _spark dancer_ casts either [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) or [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), depending on whether the current core would deal [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage, as a 7th-level [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell (DC 30).

</column>

---

While looking straight down the barrel of this _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196)_, the spark gun’s magical core is visible amid several reflectors. All damage dealt by a _thundercrasher_ is sonic damage. On a critical hit, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You overload the _thundercrasher_ to emit chaotic sonic frequencies that soften earth and stone. When you next fire the _thundercrasher_ it also partially liquefies any natural earth or stone surfaces within range of its [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait, making the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thundercrasher

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blister Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4429)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 42

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Blister ammunition is loaded with alchemically processed irritants, such as pollen, pepper, and formic acid. A creature hit by activated blister ammunition must attempt a Fortitude save or begin to itch uncontrollably in addition to damage the attack normally deals. On a critical hit, increase the Fortitude DC by 2, and the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. For the duration, each time the target attempts a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action, it must attempt a DC 8 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333), losing the action on a failure. An affected creature can use a single Interact action to scratch and sneeze, allowing it to automatically pass the flat check. The effect ends early once an affected creature spends 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions scratching and sneezing. These Interact actions don’t need to be consecutive.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 42

**Price** 21 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The maximum duration is 3 rounds, and the Fortitude DC is 19.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The maximum duration is 6 rounds, and the Fortitude DC is 28.

level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blister Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blister Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4429)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 42

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Blister ammunition is loaded with alchemically processed irritants, such as pollen, pepper, and formic acid. A creature hit by activated blister ammunition must attempt a Fortitude save or begin to itch uncontrollably in addition to damage the attack normally deals. On a critical hit, increase the Fortitude DC by 2, and the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. For the duration, each time the target attempts a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action, it must attempt a DC 8 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333), losing the action on a failure. An affected creature can use a single Interact action to scratch and sneeze, allowing it to automatically pass the flat check. The effect ends early once an affected creature spends 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions scratching and sneezing. These Interact actions don’t need to be consecutive.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Freeze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4431)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 42

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Freeze ammunition carries chilling reagents that activate on contact with the target. A creature hit by activated freeze ammunition takes [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage instead of the weapon’s normal damage type, plus 2 cold [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. Hitting a 5-foot‑square surface successfully with freeze ammunition deals 2 cold splash damage and covers the space in a layer of ice. Each creature standing on the icy surface must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check or else fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). Creatures using an action to move onto the icy surface must attempt either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369). Creatures that [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawl](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) don’t need to attempt a check or save. The ice melts after 1 minute, although unusually [hot or cold temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=1901) can change this duration at the GM’s discretion. Dealing at least 1 point of fire damage to the ice removes it instantly.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Freeze Ammunition

Life shot is a special cartridge that carries a small dose of elixir of life. A creature hit by activated life shot takes no damage from the successful attack, instead receiving healing and gaining an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 1 minute. On a critical hit, roll the healing received twice and take the better result (this is a fortune effect). A target willing to be hit by this attack is flat-footed against it.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4433)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ooze ammunition is a capsule containing a sticky substance. If you hit a creature with activated ooze ammunition, it deals acid damage instead of its normal damage type, and the creature then takes the listed penalty to Speed and persistent acid damage until it ends the effects. On a critical hit, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round in addition to the other effects. The target can end the effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the sticky foam. Other creatures can provide the actions, although doing so deals half the ammunition’s [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to the assisting creature. A creature that ends the effect still takes the persistent damage that turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ooze Ammunition (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 1d4 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and imposes a –5-foot penalty to Speed, and the Escape DC is 16.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ooze Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4433)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ooze ammunition is a capsule containing a sticky substance. If you hit a creature with activated ooze ammunition, it deals acid damage instead of its normal damage type, and the creature then takes the listed penalty to Speed and persistent acid damage until it ends the effects. On a critical hit, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round in addition to the other effects. The target can end the effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the sticky foam. Other creatures can provide the actions, although doing so deals half the ammunition’s [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to the assisting creature. A creature that ends the effect still takes the persistent damage that turn.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rusting Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4434)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rusting ammunition is made using a [rust monster’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=354) saliva and, when activated, deals damage to objects or creatures primarily made of metal. The target takes [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) for a duration determined by the type of ammunition used. A creature that drops to 0 Hit Points while taking this persistent damage crumbles into fine powder; its gear remains. The ammunition’s type determines the maximum amount of an object that’s destroyed.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the saliva of a [rust monster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=354).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Rusting Ammunition (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 3d8 persistent damage for up to 5 rounds. For an object, the ammunition destroys a 5-foot cube.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Rusting Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 43

**Price** 3,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ammunition deals 4d8 persistent damage for up to 6 rounds. For an object, the ammunition destroys a 10-foot cube.
name: Rusting Ammunition

**Craft Requirements** Supply the saliva of a [rust monster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=354).

    level="1"
    right="Item 15+"
    pfs="Standard"

[Blightburn Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4435)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Blightburn bombs have radioactive materials sealed inside flasks treated with lead. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals poison damage, [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and poison [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage, according to the bomb’s type. A creature that takes the persistent poison damage deals the splash damage again from its current position as the radiation continues to harm nearby creatures. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute. Blightburn bombs also expose the primary target to [blightburn sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21) at the listed Fortitude DC.

</column>

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d6 poison damage, 3d4 persistent poison damage, and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 34.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boulder Seed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4436)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bomb is made of volatile fluids that rapidly expand and harden when exposed to air. A boulder seed grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals bludgeoning damage and bludgeoning splash damage, according to the bomb’s type. When activated, the bomb fills a 5-foot cube with hardened foam, which has stats according to the bomb’s type and which pushes a primary target of a certain size or smaller occupying that space 5 feet away from you. On a critical hit, the target also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The splash zone fills with rubble, creating [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). The “boulder” the bomb creates fails all saving throws and loses 1 Hardness per round, disintegrating into fine powder when the boulder’s Hardness is reduced to 0. At that time, the difficult terrain the bomb created also disappears.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boulder Seed
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d4 bludgeoning damage plus 3 bludgeoning splash damage. It creates a boulder as hard as wood (Hardness 5, HP 20) that pushes Medium or smaller targets.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boulder Seed (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

**Price** 3,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d4 bludgeoning damage plus 4 bludgeoning splash damage. It creates a boulder as hard as stone (Hardness 10, HP 40) that pushes Large or smaller targets
name: Boulder Seed

---

Lodestone bombs hold reactive ionized minerals preserved in a dormant state until broken. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals force damage and force splash damage, according to the bomb’s type. In addition, a target made of metal, wearing metal armor, or using metal weapons takes [persistent force damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 while taking the persistent damage. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

Lodestone bombs hold reactive ionized minerals preserved in a dormant state until broken. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals force damage and force splash damage, according to the bomb’s type. In addition, a target made of metal, wearing metal armor, or using metal weapons takes [persistent force damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 while taking the persistent damage. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

>
[Shatterstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4438)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A shatterstone is a small ceramic orb, much like a [thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288). Inside are reactive agents that set up an intense field of sonic vibration when the stone breaks. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals sonic damage and sonic splash damage, according to the bomb’s type. Much of the sound is ultrasonic, and creatures with sonic weakness that take damage from the bomb must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw at the listed DC or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) until the end of their next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shatterstone
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

**Price** 380 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d6 sonic damage and 3 sonic splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 30.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shatterstone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 44

**Price** 3,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d6 sonic damage and 4 sonic splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 40.
name: Shatterstone

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- Skunk bombs are made from the concentrated odors of xulgaths, hezrous, and other creatures with natural or supernatural stench. The bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals poison damage and poison splash damage. Any creature hit by the bomb or in its splash area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw with a DC based on the bomb’s type. Creatures in the splash area treat the results of their saving throw as one step better. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The target is sickened 1 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 while sickened.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded for 1 round, sickened 2, and slowed 1 while sickened. Creatures sickened by the bomb emit an odor that lasts 10 minutes after the sickened condition ends (or 1 hour if they were also blinded). The odor can be removed or neutralized by using [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) or similar magic or by spending 10 minutes scrubbing with ample soap and water. While the odor lasts, creatures within 30 feet can smell the target, enabling even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to Track the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise scent doubles the range at which it can detect the target using this scent. Skunk Bomb (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 4 gp --- The bomb deals 1d4 poison damage and 1 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 15. Skunk Bomb (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 12 gp --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 poison damage and 2 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 17. Skunk Bomb (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 240 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 poison damage and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 28. Skunk Bomb (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 2,400 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 poison damage and 4 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 37.
name: Skunk Bomb

Any creature hit by the bomb or in its splash area must attempt a Fortitude saving throw with a DC based on the bomb’s type. Creatures in the splash area treat the results of their saving throw as one step better. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The target is sickened 1 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 while sickened.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded for 1 round, sickened 2, and slowed 1 while sickened. Creatures sickened by the bomb emit an odor that lasts 10 minutes after the sickened condition ends (or 1 hour if they were also blinded). The odor can be removed or neutralized by using [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) or similar magic or by spending 10 minutes scrubbing with ample soap and water. While the odor lasts, creatures within 30 feet can smell the target, enabling even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to Track the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise scent doubles the range at which it can detect the target using this scent. Skunk Bomb (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 12 gp --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 poison damage and 2 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 17. Skunk Bomb (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 240 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 poison damage and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 28. Skunk Bomb (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45 **Price** 2,400 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 poison damage and 4 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 37.
name: Skunk Bomb (Moderate)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trueshape Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4440)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Concentrated wolfsbane and other anti-shapechanger reagents fill trueshape bombs. These bombs grant an item bonus to attack rolls and deal poison damage, [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and poison splash damage, according to their type. If the primary target is under the effects of a [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657) or [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect, it must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw at the bomb’s listed DC, or else the effects end and the creature returns to its normal form. Targets taking persistent poison damage from this bomb must succeed at another Fortitude saving throw at the same DC to change shape using a morph or polymorph effect. The persistent damage can last up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
Trueshape Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 45

**Price** 375 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d6 poison damage, 3d4 persistent poison damage, and 3 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 30.

level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Trueshape Bomb (Greater)
</title>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d6 poison damage, 4d4 persistent poison damage, and 4 poison splash damage. The Fortitude DC is 40.
name: Trueshape Bomb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assassin Vine Wine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4441)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafting wine from [assassin vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555) berries is a dangerous process, but the resulting ruby-red drink is rich and heady. Alchemical reagents added during fermentation concentrate the vine’s connection to its environment and impart them onto the imbiber. For 1 minute after drinking a glass of assassin vine wine, you have [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) at a range of 30 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Assassin Vine Wine
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Assassin Vine Wine (Aged)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Assassin vine wine that has been aged for several years— or artificially aged with alchemy—develops both a greater complexity of flavor and a stronger connection to an assassin vine’s bond with nature. When you drink the wine, vegetation in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you begins to writhe, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the start of your next turn.
name: Assassin Vine Wine

When you drink the wine, vegetation in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you begins to writhe, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the start of your next turn.
name: Assassin Vine Wine

---

Crafting wine from [assassin vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555) berries is a dangerous process, but the resulting ruby-red drink is rich and heady. Alchemical reagents added during fermentation concentrate the vine’s connection to its environment and impart them onto the imbiber. For 1 minute after drinking a glass of assassin vine wine, you have [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) at a range of 30 feet.

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Auric Noodles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4442)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Auric noodles are boiled, then pan-fried and tossed with sliced vegetables and a sticky, savory sauce incorporating alchemical reagents. After you eat the noodles, they boost your ability to sense magic for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), and you can move at full speed while using the [Detect Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624) exploration activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Auric Noodles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cinnamon Seers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4443)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Zippy, alchemically treated cinnamon suffuses cinnamon seers, a rock candy with a lively taste that provides a mental boost. A cinnamon seer remains in your mouth for 1 hour, its stimulating flavor granting you a +1 item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge. 

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Trigger** You gain no information from a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check; **Effect** Reroll the triggering check. If this was a secret check, the GM rerolls rather than you; the candy doesn’t give you any insight into what the GM rolled, so in that case, you’re rerolling based only on guesswork. The seer becomes inert. You become temporarily immune to cinnamon seers until the next time you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cinnamon Seers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cooperative Waffles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4444)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Flash-cooked on a waffle iron and drizzled with alchemical syrups and compound butter, cooperative waffles bolster the supportive spirit of those who share the batch. You can split the waffles with one other creature. After you both eat half of the waffles to Activate them, when one of you uses Follow the Expert to follow the other, the circumstance bonus granted is 1 higher. The waffles’ bonus lasts 24 hours or until you next make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first. You can only be linked to one creature in this way at a time; if either of you eats cooperative waffles again, the effect of your previous waffles ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
Cooperative Waffles
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cooperative Waffles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The benefit of the waffles also applies when one of you successfully [Aids](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) a skill check the other attempts.
name: Cooperative Waffles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crackling Bubble Gum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4445)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tangy gum cracks and pops in your mouth as you chew it. While you’re chewing crackling bubble gum, for up to 10 minutes, you have an item bonus to saving throws against [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and sonic effects.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Effect** You blow a bubble with the gum until it pops, after which it becomes inert. The pop deals sonic damage to all creatures in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also bound with sticky gum, taking a –10-foot item penalty to its Speed for 1 minute. The creature can remove the gum with a total of 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. These actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

</column>

<title

    right="Item 5+"

>
[Crackling Bubble Gum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4445)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tangy gum cracks and pops in your mouth as you chew it. While you’re chewing crackling bubble gum, for up to 10 minutes, you have an item bonus to saving throws against [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and sonic effects.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Effect** You blow a bubble with the gum until it pops, after which it becomes inert. The pop deals sonic damage to all creatures in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also bound with sticky gum, taking a –10-foot item penalty to its Speed for 1 minute. The creature can remove the gum with a total of 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. These actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

>
[Delve Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4446)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 155 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

If fried, a [bulette’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=61) thick forefoot scales render into crunchy snacks. Alchemists add reagents to the frying oil to enhance the scales’ properties and flavor. For 1 minute after eating a delve scale, you gain a burrow Speed of 15 feet and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Delve Scale

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diplomat's Charcuterie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4447)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 47

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A common sight at political gatherings, a diplomat’s charcuterie has fine meats, cheeses, nuts, fruits, and other finger foods mixed with reagents to engender friendly feelings between those consuming them. Contents of the plate vary by chef and the intended palates, from the hearty sausages and hard cheese of charcuterie from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to the hot-pepper cheese curds and smoked almonds of Thuvian platters. After Activating the charcuterie by eating it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302). These bonuses last 24 hours or until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diplomat's Charcuterie

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Price** 27 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Containing neither eggs nor cream, an egg cream fizz contains milk or nut milk, sparkling water, and flavored syrup, frothed and chilled. Upon drinking, you feel lighter and more buoyant, gaining a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed for 10 minutes. During this time, you also gain another effect determined by the drink’s syrup, which is chosen when the drink is created.
  • **Chocolate** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371), and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372).
  • **Strawberry** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381).
  • **Vanilla** You can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
name: Egg Cream Fizz

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Firefoot Popcorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4449)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you consume firefoot popcorn, for 1 minute, you can Leap double the normal distance. You can also attempt to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) as a single action. If you do, you don’t perform the initial [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) (nor do you fall if you don’t Stride 10 feet). 

Each time you Leap 10 feet or more, your feet make a popping sound that can be heard from at least 100 feet, and the space you left fills with waves of intense heat. For 1 minute, that square is [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368) that deals 1d6 fire damage to any creature that moves into it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Firefoot Popcorn

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- A fury cocktail is a fortifying ginger beer spiked with rum and a mixer. It’s rumored to have originated from a barbarian-themed festival in a popular mead hall specializing in alchemical beverages. **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to melee attack rolls and an additional effect depending on the additive chosen when the brew is created.
  • **Animalistic** Lemon juice and powdered claws or talons are added to the cocktail. You gain an [unarmed attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2191) in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group of your choice between a [jaws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage or a claw attack that has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait and deals 1d4 slashing damage.
  • **Double** This cocktail is just stronger, with more ginger and more rum. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 1 to physical damage. The resistance increases to 2 for a moderate fury cocktail or 5 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Mournful** A few flower petals add a powerful aroma to the drink. You gain resistance 1 to negative damage, or resistance 1 to positive damage if you have [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83). The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Skeptical** A splash of bitters gives the drink a more complex flavor. You gain a +1 item bonus to saves against magic. This bonus is +2 for a moderate fury cocktail or +3 for a greater fury cocktail.
  • **Titanic** Yuzu juice and powdered giant hair are added to this cocktail. If you’re Medium or smaller, you gain the following effects: you become Large, are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, and increase your reach by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out Tiny).
  • **Wyrmhide** Pomegranate juice and elemental reagents are added to the cocktail. You gain resistance 1 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and poison damage. The resistance increases to 5 for a moderate fury cocktail or 10 for a greater fury cocktail.
**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves. Fury Cocktail (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Price** 15 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 10 minutes. Fury Cocktail (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Price** 360 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour. Fury Cocktail (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48 **Price** 4,000 gp --- You gain a +4 item bonus to melee attack rolls, and the duration is 1 hour.
name: Fury Cocktail

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Galvanic Chew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4451)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 48

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A galvanic chew is a processed ball of dried electric eel, roasted until chewy and coated in spicy, reagent-infused powder that tingles with electricity as you chew. For up to 1 hour, you have [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to electricity.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) **Trigger** You’re hit by a melee attack or touched by a creature; **Effect** You channel electricity into the triggering creature, which must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (or stunned 2 on a critical failure). The chew becomes inert. You’re then temporarily immune to galvanic chews for 24 hours or until the next time you make your daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Galvanic Chew

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ginger Chew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4452)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This chewy ginger candy aids digestion and soothes unsettled stomachs. A ginger chew lasts for 1 hour and grants you a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves against being sickened. 

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Effect** Reduce your [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) value by 1, if any. The chew becomes inert.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ginger Chew

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- A popular choice for [investigators](/Classes.aspx?ID=59) studying alchemy, insight coffee is infused with alchemical flavoring during percolation. For 1 hour after you drink an insight coffee, you use d8s instead of d6s for your extra damage from the strategic strike class feature, if you have it. You also gain an item bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) with a skill determined by the blend chosen when the item is crafted.
  • **Double Coffee** [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)
  • **Hazelnut** [ Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)
  • **Mocha** [ Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)
  • **Vanilla** [ Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)
  • **Pumpkin Spice** [ Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)
  • **Toffee** [ Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)
Insight Coffee (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 40 gp --- The item bonus is +2. Insight Coffee (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 375 gp --- The item bonus is +3. Insight Coffee (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49 **Price** 7,500 gp --- The item bonus is +4.
name: Insight Coffee

</title>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Journeybread contains a mix of fruits, nuts, and grains with an alchemical boost. Eating one journeybread provides all the food and water you need for a day. If you [subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) on nothing else for a week, you become temporarily immune to journeybread until you eat real food and drink water normally for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 1"

Journeybread
</title>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the benefits of journeybread, power journeybread grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), and [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and to Fortitude saving throws against being [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). The number of minutes you can Hustle changes to your Constitution modifier × 20. These benefits last for 4 hours.

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Journeybread contains a mix of fruits, nuts, and grains with an alchemical boost. Eating one journeybread provides all the food and water you need for a day. If you [subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) on nothing else for a week, you become temporarily immune to journeybread until you eat real food and drink water normally for 24 hours.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mender's Soup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4455)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hot, broth-based soup typically contains tubers, leeks, zesty spices, reagents, and if desired, the meat of livestock. Civic authorities commission batches of mender’s soup for workers if a tricky job is on the agenda. After you eat the soup, its effects last 24 hours or until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) and restore an additional 5 Hit Points to items you successfully Repair during this time. 

If you eat mender’s soup over the entire period required to attempt a Crafting check to Craft (typically a minimum of eating the soup each day for 4 days), the +1 bonus from the soup can be applied to that check, too.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mender's Soup

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Missive Mint](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4456)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 49

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This white mint with a chalky coating appears to be a normal candy unless someone examining it succeeds at a DC 15 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check to [Identify Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2386). If the crafter has the powerful alchemy class feature, this DC is their class DC instead, if it’s higher. The mint’s crafter can imbue a missive mint with a message containing up to 25 words while creating it. Someone who consumes the missive mint hears the message in a fizzing voice as the mint’s coating bubbles away, which takes the same amount of time as it would to speak the message. The mint’s eater has no way of knowing who the original sender was, what they sound like, or who the message was intended for.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Missive Mint

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Not only are [owlbear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=328) eggs delicious when boiled, but when infused with a mix of alchemical reagents, they also make you emit a long screech much like an owlbear’s. All creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 23 Will save. Regardless of the result, creatures in the area are temporarily immune to this screech for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round.
name: Owlbear Egg

A phantom roll contains vegetables, greens, and fine, clear noodles, all wrapped in transparent, edible starch paper and alchemically treated and laced with a tangy sauce. Upon eating the roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks you attempt during the [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=2622) exploration activity. You can also Avoid Notice at full Speed or combine it with [Investigate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2627) or Scout while moving at half Speed. These effects expire 24 hours after you eat the roll or when you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), whichever comes first.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a special mixture of honey and alchemical reagents, poison fizz is a zesty, sweet rock candy that pops and crackles in your mouth. For 1 hour, you have an item bonus to saving throws against poison and being [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87). 

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Effect** You bite the poison fizz to release its poisonous liquid center and spray green mist in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). This deals poison damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of your next turn and is then temporarily immune to being blinded by poison fizz for 1 hour.

</column>

<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a special mixture of honey and alchemical reagents, poison fizz is a zesty, sweet rock candy that pops and crackles in your mouth. For 1 hour, you have an item bonus to saving throws against poison and being [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87). 

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Effect** You bite the poison fizz to release its poisonous liquid center and spray green mist in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). This deals poison damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of your next turn and is then temporarily immune to being blinded by poison fizz for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pucker Pickle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4460)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp and pungent, but tasty, pucker pickles were created by [goblin ](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62) [alchemists](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) working to avoid being eaten by larger creatures. For 1 hour after eating a pucker pickle, you smell slightly of pickle, but you have a horrendous taste. Once a creature hits you with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) using an attack that allows it to taste you, such as a [jaws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) Strike, it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to further attacks against you that allow it to taste you, including attacks like [Grappling](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [Tripping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) you using its jaws or [Swallowing you Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77). Creatures, especially animals, often choose other targets after tasting you. Any creature that [Engulfs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) you or Swallows you Whole is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. If it spends an action retching to reduce the sickened condition, you can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as a reaction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pucker Pickle

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Saboteur's Friend](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4461)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The euphemistically named saboteur’s friend looks, smells, and tastes like an appetizing chocolate square. You lace the chocolate with reagents that induce a strong laxative effect. Saboteur’s friend is useful for incapacitating rather than dealing lasting harm. Unlike some poisons, saboteur’s friend can have its [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) condition reduced (but changing to a higher or lower stage after a save applies any sickened condition listed for that stage, as normal).

**Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 1 hour; **Stage 1** sickened 1 (10 minutes); **Stage 2** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 and sickened 2 (10 minutes); **Stage 3** enfeebled 1, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and sickened 3 (10 minutes)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Saboteur's Friend

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scholar's Drop](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4462)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

University students in Katapesh first used the scholar’s drop to gain an edge over their academic rivals, but the candy has since spread across Golarion. The flavor of this hard, sugar‑coated candy is highly refreshing and based on lemon and green tea. For 1 hour, you gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects that could render you [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Requirements** You’re fatigued; **Effect** Ignore the effects of the fatigued condition for 10 minutes. The drop’s other effects end for you, and when the 10 minutes are up, you’re temporarily immune to scholar’s drops for 1 hour. If you use this effect three times in a single day, you become temporarily immune to scholar’s drops entirely until you get a full night’s rest.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scholar's Drop

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silver Crescent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4463)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A piquant tamarind and chili-lime flavor infuses a silver crescent, which was first created to aid those battling the undead. For 1 hour, you shed cool, white light like a torch, and you gain an item bonus to saving throws against [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) effects according to the crescent’s type. While shedding this light, you can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) if you’re visible, and if you’re [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), you’re concealed instead rather than being undetected.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Effect** A ray of light descends on a 5-foot square of your choice within 120 feet. Any creature in that space takes positive damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn on a failed save. This is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. The silver crescent becomes inert.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51 **Price** 45 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Hot tea with a comforting, flowery aroma, a soothing toddy grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects and against effects with a trait determined by the liquor mixed into the tea when it’s created. These benefits last for 1 hour.
  • **Amaretto** [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)
  • **Limoncello** [Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)
  • **Whiskey** [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)
name: Soothing Toddy

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sprite Apple](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4465)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sparkling candy coating covers a sprite apple. For 10 minutes after consuming a sprite apple, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet). While shedding this light, you can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) if you’re visible, and if you’re [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), you’re concealed instead rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96). The light matches the vibrant color of the apple’s candy coating. Creatures in the bright light are subject to another effect, depending on the type of apple.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

**Price** 26 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel more in tune with nature, gaining a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=85) checks and to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks attempted against animals or plants (if they have the wild empathy class feature or some other ability to make such checks).

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel kind and inviting, taking a –1 item penalty to their Will DC against [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks.

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sprite Apple (Pink)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel a burst of creativity, gaining a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks.

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Creatures in the bright light feel a sense of calm, gaining a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tracker's Stew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4466)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>10 minutes (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Alchemical reagents add punch to tracker’s stew: a rich, fiery mixture of tomatoes, ground nuts, onions, and tubers, often with poultry added. It’s usually served with or over rice or noodles. Once you’ve eaten the stew, it improves your ability to [sense](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) and follow tracks for 24 hours or until you make your next daily preparations, whichever comes first. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2407) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408). You can do either while moving at full speed or both while moving at half speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tracker's Stew
