**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Based on a Thassilonian recipe, warding punch is made from red or white wine combined with fruits, other alcohols, and alchemical reagents. Its alchemical properties provide protection against a specific school of magic depending on the ingredients chosen when it’s created, granting a +1 item bonus to saving throws against spells from that school for 10 minutes.
  • **Beer or Cider** [Transmutation](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=8)
  • **Brown Spirits** [Necromancy](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7)
  • **Clear Spirits** [Evocation](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=5)
  • **Fruit Cordials** [Abjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=1)
  • **Fruit Juice** [Illusion](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=6)
  • **Nut Liqueurs** [Conjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=2)
  • **Spiced Spirits** [Enchantment](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=4)
name: Warding Punch

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Witch's Finger](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4468)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 51

**Price** 11 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Shaped like a pointed, gnarled finger on a stick, witch’s finger is a frozen treat imbued with berries that lend it a blood-red hue. A popular tale claims Irriseni winter [witches](/Classes.aspx?ID=38) created this dessert, but the story is apocryphal; an enterprising ice cream shop owner in New Stetven invented the treat and, as a marketing ploy, the myth surrounding it. Taking a bite makes you feel warm. For 1 hour, you have cold [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 3, and for 8 hours, the treat negates the damage you would take from severe environmental cold and reduces the damage you take from [extreme cold to that of severe cold.](/Rules.aspx?ID=1901)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Witch's Finger

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Repellent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4469)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal repellent is a noxious alchemical substance that makes you repulsive to a certain kind of animal. You can, for example, make [ape](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=363) repellent, [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370) repellent, or [snake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=473) repellent, but not a repellent that affects all animals. The repellent doesn’t work on creatures that are similar to the kind of animal, but not actually animals—for example, bear repellent wouldn’t work on werebears. Animal repellent is ineffective against animals with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher, such as awakened animals. When you initially learn the formula for animal repellent, you learn the formulas for all common animals. If no animals of a kind are common, such as sea serpents, you must learn the formula for that kind separately, and it has the same rarity as the least-rare creature of that kind. 

You Activate animal repellent by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, or by spreading it on a surface in an area up to 10 feet square. The repellent lasts for 24 hours or until scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work (consisting of multiple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions). The designated kind of animal can smell the repellent from three times as far away as normal.

The designated kind of creature typically avoids the coated creature or area, which smells foul and feels unnerving to it. If in combat or otherwise distressed, the creature can approach within 20 feet of the affected creature or area only if it succeeds at a Will save with a DC determined by the type of animal repellent. Once it succeeds at this save, the animal is temporarily immune for 1 minute. If the animal attacks in a way that causes it to taste or ingest the repellent, it must succeed at a Fortitude save at the listed DC or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (or sickened 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal repellent is a noxious alchemical substance that makes you repulsive to a certain kind of animal. You can, for example, make [ape](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=363) repellent, [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370) repellent, or [snake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=473) repellent, but not a repellent that affects all animals. The repellent doesn’t work on creatures that are similar to the kind of animal, but not actually animals—for example, bear repellent wouldn’t work on werebears. Animal repellent is ineffective against animals with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher, such as awakened animals. When you initially learn the formula for animal repellent, you learn the formulas for all common animals. If no animals of a kind are common, such as sea serpents, you must learn the formula for that kind separately, and it has the same rarity as the least-rare creature of that kind. 

You Activate animal repellent by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, or by spreading it on a surface in an area up to 10 feet square. The repellent lasts for 24 hours or until scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work (consisting of multiple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions). The designated kind of animal can smell the repellent from three times as far away as normal.

The designated kind of creature typically avoids the coated creature or area, which smells foul and feels unnerving to it. If in combat or otherwise distressed, the creature can approach within 20 feet of the affected creature or area only if it succeeds at a Will save with a DC determined by the type of animal repellent. Once it succeeds at this save, the animal is temporarily immune for 1 minute. If the animal attacks in a way that causes it to taste or ingest the repellent, it must succeed at a Fortitude save at the listed DC or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (or sickened 2 on a critical failure).

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Dye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4470)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Camouflage dye uses a variety of alchemically treated paints and crushed crystals to make the user particularly hard to distinguish from their surroundings. When you Activate the dye by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach, the target and its clothing change colors, blending into their surroundings until the target makes a sudden movement. The target can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424) for 10 minutes. If the target uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) or moves at more than half its Speed, after that action is completed, the effects of camouflage powder end and the creature ceases to be [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Colorful Coating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4471)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It’s possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM’s discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Blue)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that’s loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface’s collapse. 

This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for Athletics checks to Force Open the coated surface.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a +2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object’s [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating’s surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as [_reverse gravity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=261).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 20) or spending 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square’s surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by slipperiness, such as from [ice](/Rules.aspx?ID=2774) or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces uneven ground to greater difficult terrain. The coating can’t affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface’s terrain.

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Colorful Coating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4471)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It’s possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM’s discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Blue)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that’s loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface’s collapse. 

This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for Athletics checks to Force Open the coated surface.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a +2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object’s [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating’s surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as [_reverse gravity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=261).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 20) or spending 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square’s surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by slipperiness, such as from [ice](/Rules.aspx?ID=2774) or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces uneven ground to greater difficult terrain. The coating can’t affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface’s terrain.

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating (Blue)

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Blue colorful coating contains bonding compounds that firm up a surface, making it more stable and more supportive of weight. When applied to material that’s loose or unstable, the square can hold twice as much weight before giving way. The coating also slows any collapse, making it easier to avoid and granting a +1 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws in reaction to the coated surface’s collapse. 

This coating can shore up a wall, ceiling, door, or similar opening, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC for Athletics checks to Force Open the coated surface.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Green colorful coating dries into a low-friction surface. It makes the square slippery, making it both [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) with a DC to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) of 20. A creature that [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Crawls](/Actions.aspx?ID=2293) doesn’t have to attempt to Balance. 

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling harder to climb. It imposes a +2 circumstance penalty to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.

Yellow colorful coating and green colorful coating have opposite effects. If they’re both applied to the same surface, they negate one another.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Indigo)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Indigo colorful coating contains agents that destroy weaker objects. When applied to a surface or object, the coating treats the object’s [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) as 2 lower and deals 1d4 acid damage to the object each round. The damage this coating deals remains after the coating becomes inert.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Orange)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating’s surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as [_reverse gravity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=261).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 20) or spending 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square’s surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by slipperiness, such as from [ice](/Rules.aspx?ID=2774) or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces uneven ground to greater difficult terrain. The coating can’t affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface’s terrain.

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating (Orange)

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coatings come in different colors, each with a different special effect. A bottle of colorful coating contains enough to slather over one 5-foot square within your reach or space and is made with a special dispenser that enables you to coat the surface using only one hand. It’s possible to use the coating on any surface that can be painted (subject to the GM’s discretion). Colorful coating dries instantly, and its effect in the square you coated lasts for 1 minute. After that time, the coating turns to fine, inert powder, returning the square to its original condition unless otherwise noted.

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Orange colorful coating contains a mixture of acidic substances. As it dries, caustic ingredients rise to the coating’s surface. The square becomes [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368), dealing 1d4 acid damage to any creature that moves through it. A creature can take this acid damage only once per round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Red colorful coating contains rubbery compounds, turning any surface it covers into a trampoline. A creature or object that falls onto a square coated with red colorful coating takes no falling damage and bounces half again as high as the original fall. If the creature fell straight down, then with several bounces, it comes safely to a stop. If it was moving horizontally, its bounce continues in the same direction, with a distance determined by the GM. If the coating is on a wall, a creature [pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) or [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) into the wall bounces back half again as far and avoids taking any damage from being pushed into the wall. On a ceiling, red colorful coating is useful only in unusual circumstances, such as [_reverse gravity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=261).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Violet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Violet colorful coating contains compounds that become goopy and sticky. A square of this coating is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Any creature that enters the square must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –15-foot penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. On a critical failure, the creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in the square until the end of its next turn. The target can end these effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 20) or spending 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can help by providing the actions.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Colorful Coating (Yellow)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 53

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Yellow colorful coating contains a variety of compounds that fill small gaps and harden into a flat surface that increases friction, making it much easier to move on the square’s surface. On the affected surface, the coating reduces [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by slipperiness, such as from [ice](/Rules.aspx?ID=2774) or grease; this eliminates difficult terrain, reduces greater difficult terrain to difficult terrain, and reduces uneven ground to greater difficult terrain. The coating can’t affect difficult terrain caused by features or effects not part of the surface, such as crowds, underbrush, wind, magic, and so on. The GM determines if yellow colorful coating can affect a surface’s terrain.

The coating also makes a coated wall or ceiling easier to climb. It provides a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the coated surface.
name: Colorful Coating (Yellow)

</row>

</column>

---

[Sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) creatures have difficulty swallowing, so you can Activate emetic paste by applying it to your skin or that of a sickened creature within reach, typically on the throat. The paste makes it easy for the sickened creature to purge, granting it an immediate Fortitude save to reduce its sickened condition. The paste grants the target an item bonus to that save and to all saving throws to reduce the sickened condition for 1 hour.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Gearbinder oil comes in a sealed pump that can squirt the oil a short distance. The oil is designed to flow through complex mechanisms and, agitated through mechanical action, foam up and form a paste that binds the works. The oil is effective against articulated [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563) and machinery, including many constructs, [clockworks](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575), and [mechanical](/Traits.aspx?ID=646) hazards. You apply the oil to the target you want to bind, which must be within 10 feet of you. After the oil is applied, at the end of any round during which the target took an action with the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait, it must attempt a Fortitude save against a DC determined by the oil’s type. A mechanism that’s [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 2 or more by gearbinder oil also can’t use reactions. Gearbinder oil functions for up to 6 rounds before becoming an inert, oily residue. **Critical Success** The oil becomes inert, and the effect ends.
**Success** The target reduces its [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition by 1. If the slowed condition’s value is 0, the effect ends.
**Failure** The target increases its [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition by 1, to a maximum of slowed 3.
**Critical Failure** The target increases its slowed condition by 2, to a maximum of slowed 3. Gearbinder Oil (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 45 gp --- The DC for saves is 20. Gearbinder Oil (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 390 gp --- The DC for saves is 29. Gearbinder Oil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 4,500 gp --- The DC for saves is 38.
name: Gearbinder Oil

**Failure** The target increases its [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition by 1, to a maximum of slowed 3.
**Critical Failure** The target increases its slowed condition by 2, to a maximum of slowed 3. Gearbinder Oil (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 45 gp --- The DC for saves is 20. Gearbinder Oil (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 390 gp --- The DC for saves is 29. Gearbinder Oil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 4,500 gp --- The DC for saves is 38.

Healing vapor is a substance that accelerates natural recovery processes by dispersing a mist infused with a variety of reagents typically used for healing and recovery. When deployed from a sealed container, the vapors fill a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), last for 10 minutes, and can affect up to four living creatures at one time. Any creatures beyond the first four gain no benefit, though if a creature leaves before the duration is over, a new creature that enters can benefit from the mist. A creature benefiting from the vapors regains a number of Hit Points based on the vapor’s type. While affected, a creature also gains an item bonus to saving throws against [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). If the areas of more than one healing vapor overlap, only the strongest applies to creatures inside overlapping areas. Strong wind disperses the mist, rendering it ineffective while the wind blows.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Sealed in a packet so it can be released in a pressurized puff, implosion dust causes amorphous creatures to compress and shrink by hardening and even evaporating the liquid components of their physical forms. It’s effective at weakening [water elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=412), [air elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=408), [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), and other creatures the GM determines are similarly amorphous, which can even include particularly gelatinous [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523). You release the dust toward one creature within 5 feet of you, which must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. Implosion dust functions for up to 6 rounds. It then becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size. **Critical Success** The dust becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size.
**Success** The target increases in size one step, up to its normal size. If it’s at its normal size after the increase, the effect ends.
**Failure** The target decreases in size one step, to a minimum of Tiny. The reach of each of its Strikes decreases by 5 feet, to a minimum of 5 feet if the creature is Small or larger, or a minimum of 0 feet if the creature is Tiny. Shrinking also imposes a status penalty to the creature’s physical melee damage. This penalty is –2 for every step lower than its normal size the creature is.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target decreases in size by two steps. Implosion Dust (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 40 gp --- The save DC is 20. Implosion Dust (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55 **Price** 370 gp --- The save DC is 29. Implosion Dust (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55 **Price** 4,100 gp --- The save DC is 38.
name: Implosion Dust

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Sealed in a packet so it can be released in a pressurized puff, implosion dust causes amorphous creatures to compress and shrink by hardening and even evaporating the liquid components of their physical forms. It’s effective at weakening [water elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=412), [air elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=408), [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), and other creatures the GM determines are similarly amorphous, which can even include particularly gelatinous [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523). You release the dust toward one creature within 5 feet of you, which must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. Implosion dust functions for up to 6 rounds. It then becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size. **Critical Success** The dust becomes inert, and the creature returns to its normal size.
**Success** The target increases in size one step, up to its normal size. If it’s at its normal size after the increase, the effect ends.
**Failure** The target decreases in size one step, to a minimum of Tiny. The reach of each of its Strikes decreases by 5 feet, to a minimum of 5 feet if the creature is Small or larger, or a minimum of 0 feet if the creature is Tiny. Shrinking also imposes a status penalty to the creature’s physical melee damage. This penalty is –2 for every step lower than its normal size the creature is.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target decreases in size by two steps. Implosion Dust (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 54 **Price** 40 gp --- The save DC is 20. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Revealing Mist](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4476)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Kept in an airtight spray bottle, revealing mist is an alchemical concoction that creates a sticky and clinging mist of chemicals in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) when sprayed. It doesn’t affect visibility but causes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures in the area to be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96). Revealing mist is ineffective in water or in areas with other factors affecting the spread of the mist, as determined by the GM. It remains in the area for 1 minute or until any significant wind disperses it, whichever comes first.

</column>

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Revealing Mist (Greater)
</title>

[Sense-Dulling Hood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4477)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes, an enemy’s dangerous special ability makes relatively standard sensory capabilities a liability. From [otyugh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=327) stench to a [harpy’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046) song, plenty of creatures use their prey’s senses against them. A sense-dulling hood is a wide, single-use mask designed to be pulled from an airtight package and slipped over the head. The mask grants you an item bonus to saving throws against [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effects for a time, according to the mask’s type. Since it dulls your senses without depriving you of them, the mask also imposes a –1 penalty to rolls and checks using [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) for the same duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Soothing Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4478)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Soothing powders are remedies made to stop a particular type of [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). Each damage type requires a different formula, with the most popular being bleed soothing powder, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) soothing powder, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) soothing powder, and [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) soothing powder. You Activate soothing powder by sprinkling it on yourself or another creature within reach. The target can immediately attempt a new [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove persistent damage the powder works against. This powder lowers the DC to 10, as normal for a particularly appropriate type of help.

</column>

Soothing powders are remedies made to stop a particular type of [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). Each damage type requires a different formula, with the most popular being bleed soothing powder, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) soothing powder, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) soothing powder, and [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) soothing powder. You Activate soothing powder by sprinkling it on yourself or another creature within reach. The target can immediately attempt a new [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove persistent damage the powder works against. This powder lowers the DC to 10, as normal for a particularly appropriate type of help.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sparkler](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4479)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 55

**Price** 4 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Sparklers are common in Tian Xia and Vudra and can be Crafted by those who can specifically Craft fireworks (such as a  fireworks technician ).

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sparkler gives off colorful sparks, burning for 1 minute. It provides bright light in a 10-foot radius (and dim light for the next 10 feet). While the sparkler burns, you can use it as an improvised weapon, dealing 1 fire damage on a hit. On a critical hit, you cause the target to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. 

You can touch a sparkler to a flammable object as part of the same Interact action you use to Activate it or as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action while the sparkler is already activated. If you do so, the sparkler can ignite flammable objects the way a tindertwig does.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sparkler

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bottle contains a shrunken bird preserved with its feathers intact. When opened, the contents reconstitute into a Gargantuan effigy of a great [roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3170), which can appear in the air instead of on the ground. The roc [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) up to two creatures, then [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to 90 feet and [Releases](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) the creatures. The creatures must be within 15 feet of the roc for it to Grab them; if any of them are unwilling to be grabbed, the roc must Grapple them with a +17 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier or fail to pick them up.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [roc](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3170).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Roc

A daub of ectoplasm twitches within this glass container, faintly glowing with ghostly light. When opened it forms the echo of a departed spirit, which looks like a Medium ghost. You can throw the ampoule up to 30 feet when you Activate it. The ghost utters a final lament, forcing each living creature in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) except you to attempt a DC 18 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure).

**Craft Requirements** Supply ectoplasmic residue from a destroyed [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=418).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Coiled tentacles make it all but impossible to see anything else inside this ampoule. When opened, a Gargantuan [kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3075) bursts forth, which can appear in water instead of on the ground. Its arms attempt to grasp up to four creatures with a reach of 60 feet. The kraken repositions [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) creatures to a different space within its reach unless the target succeeds at a DC 38 Fortitude save. 

If the kraken is in water, it then releases a cloud of ink in an 80-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). This cloud has no effect outside of water. Creatures inside the cloud are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), can’t use their sense of smell, and are exposed to kraken ink poison. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3075).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kraken Bottle

  • **Shock Maw** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) 120-foot line of electricity dealing 12d6 electricity damage.
**Craft Requirements** Supply a [mukradi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3100) corpse.

[Tyrant Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4486)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 57

**Price** 175 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The body of a fearsome tyrannosaurus is shrunken and contained in this bottle, its desiccated form barely constrained within the glass. The effigy of a Gargantuan [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2923) forms when you open the bottle, causing devastation as it rampages. The tyrannosaurus [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to 40 feet. It can move through the spaces of Huge or smaller creatures and can attempt to [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81) each creature whose space it enters, dealing 2d10+12 bludgeoning damage with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. It can attempt to Trample each creature only once.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2923).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tyrant Ampoule

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 57 **Price** 250 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- Opening this vial releases a mighty gust, forming into a fearsome Huge [elemental hurricane](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2976). The elemental breathes a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of air. Each creature in the cone must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or be knocked away from the elemental. A creature knocked into a solid object stops moving and takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is pushed 10 feet.
**Failure** The creature is pushed 20 feet.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pushed 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Craft Requirements** Supply magical residue from a slain [elemental hurricane](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2976).

    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Worm Vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4488)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 57

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Opening this vial unleashes its destructive contents: a reconstituted Gargantuan [purple worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2871). The worm has two functions; choose which one to use when you Activate the item. 

**Burrow** The worm [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) up to 80 feet, leaving a tunnel behind it. It can burrow through solid stone, but if it does so it burrows 40 feet instead of 80 feet.

**Eat** The worm attempts to swallow a creature, crush it in its insides, then spit the creature out. The target must be within 15 feet, and it attempts a DC 30 Reflex save. If it fails, it takes 3d6+9 bludgeoning damage (doubled on a critical failure). The worm then spits the creature out to any empty space on the ground within 30 feet, causing the creature to take damage from a 20-foot [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352).

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [purple worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2871).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Worm Vial

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Addition Suppressant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4489)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you use a dose of addiction suppressant, it suppresses the effects of that addiction for 1 day, as if you had taken an actual dose of the drug, but without any of the drug’s effects, and it doesn’t increase the addiction DC. You also gain an item bonus against the ongoing save against the drug’s addiction depending on the type of addiction suppressant.

For every drug, a specific suppressant must be crafted— no generalized addiction suppressant yet exists. In order to create an addiction suppressant, you must begin with a dose of the drug to be suppressed, then use alchemical reagents to generate an elixir that can be used to fight that drug’s addiction. An addiction suppressant must be a higher level than the drug it is meant to suppress in order to work.

**Special** An addiction suppressant has the same rarity as the specific drug it’s crafted to suppress.

>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elixir bolsters your body’s natural defenses and ability to resist maladies that travel through or affect blood. For 10 minutes you receive the listed [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and persistent poison damage, and you lower the DC for any [flat checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end persistent bleed or persistent poison damage as if you received particularly appropriate aid. 

At the GM’s discretion, blood booster elixirs can also automatically [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) non-magical effects that specifically rely on thinning the drinker’s blood, such as a [skull peeler’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1306) anticoagulant.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Choker-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4491)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and unarmed attack damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, your reach increases by 5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, your reach increases by 5 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, your reach increases by 10 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Choker-Arm Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, your reach increases by 15 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.
name: Choker-Arm Mutagen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Choker-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4491)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and unarmed attack damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chromatic Jellyfish Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4492)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from several oils of differing hues extracted from [jellyfish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=602) and rare plants, the layers of chromatic jellyfish oil stack to form a rainbow within their vial. For 10 minutes after consuming chromatic jellyfish oil, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to precision damage and extra damage from critical hits according to the jellyfish oil’s type. While the effect lasts, you [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) caused by moving through tight spaces that aren’t tight enough to force you to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), and you can move 5 feet per round when you successfully Squeeze (or 10 feet per round on a critical success). You can also Crawl at half your Speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chromatic Jellyfish Oil (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The oil grants resistance 5.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chromatic Jellyfish Oil (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The oil grants resistance 10.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chromatic Jellyfish Oil (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 5,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The oil grants resistance 15.
name: Chromatic Jellyfish Oil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Contagion Metabolizer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4493)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Contagion metabolizers seek out toxins in the bloodstream and attempt to purify them into humors the body processes naturally. When you drink this elixir, it attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check with the listed counteract modifier to remove the highest-level [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) afflicting you. This takes longer for a disease—the counteract check doesn’t happen until 10 minutes after you drink the elixir. After drinking, you become temporarily immune to contagion metabolizers for 1 hour.

If you’re a [chirurgeon ](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=6) [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) and have powerful alchemy, you can substitute your statistics when you create a contagion metabolizer using [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2801), if your stats are higher. This replaces the counteract level with half your level rounded up and the counteract modifier with your class DC – 10.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deadweight Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4494)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to move or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) condition and can’t remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour. You can attempt to Shove or Trip creatures up to two sizes larger than you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadweight Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. You can attempt to Shove or Trip creatures up to three sizes larger than you.
name: Deadweight Mutagen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deadweight Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4494)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to move or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) condition and can’t remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Energy Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4495)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When created, this mutagen is attuned to your choice of one of four energy types: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604). When consumed, the mutagen suffuses your body with energy that spills out of you whenever you attack. At higher levels, it can even grant you the ability to unleash the energy in controlled bursts. 

**Benefit** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to the attuned energy type. Whenever you score a hit with a melee weapon, add the listed amount of damage of the attuned energy type. 

**Drawback** You gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to the other three energy types.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 4 sp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 5, add 1 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 10, add 1d4 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 15, add 1d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 2d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 8d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 20, add 2d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 3d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 12d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 32 basic Reflex save.
name: Energy Mutagen

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When created, this mutagen is attuned to your choice of one of four energy types: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604). When consumed, the mutagen suffuses your body with energy that spills out of you whenever you attack. At higher levels, it can even grant you the ability to unleash the energy in controlled bursts. 

**Benefit** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to the attuned energy type. Whenever you score a hit with a melee weapon, add the listed amount of damage of the attuned energy type. 

**Drawback** You gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to the other three energy types.

---

level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Greater)

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

---

You gain resistance 15, add 1d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 2d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 8d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

<row gap="medium">

You gain resistance 20, add 2d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 3d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 12d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 32 basic Reflex save.
